diagram_title: C Root Daemon Splice Proxy Hardening — C4 Component Diagram

systems.name: OpenCode Daemonization Runtime
systems.description: Privileged gateway plus per-user backend runtime for web and TUI access.

containers.name: Gateway Runtime
containers.description: C root gateway handling auth, routing, and splice proxying.

containers.name: Per User Backend
containers.description: Per-user opencode daemon serving HTTP, SSE, and WebSocket over Unix socket.

containers.name: Documentation Surface
containers.description: Plan artifacts, event logs, and architecture records.

components.name: JWT Validator
components.description: Parses and validates JWT claims before any authenticated proxying.

components.name: Daemon Router
components.description: Maps verified identity to the correct daemon using registry and discovery data.

components.name: Lifecycle Controller
components.description: Handles adopt, spawn, timeout, stale cleanup, and child exit handling.

components.name: Splice Proxy
components.description: Forwards byte streams between TCP clients and Unix socket daemons.

components.name: Per User Opencode Daemon
components.description: The target daemon selected after authentication and identity routing.

components.name: Documentation Sync
components.description: Updates event and architecture docs after hardening work.

relationships.description: Provides verified identity claims for daemon selection.
Invokes adopt or spawn lifecycle handling when daemon state is unresolved.
Launches or adopts the per-user daemon and yields a ready Unix socket target.
Supplies the selected daemon endpoint for byte-stream forwarding.
Proxies HTTP/SSE/WebSocket traffic to the per-user backend.
Feeds verification outcomes into event and architecture documentation.
